name: Thai Chicken and Slaw
intro: This recipe is very easy and worth the time it takes to prepare. Kids will love the sweetness of the honey. You can also omit the chicken from the recipe so that your vegetarian friends can enjoy it too.
method: In a small bowl, whisk the canola oil, white wine vinegar, honey, gingerroot, soy sauce, garlic cloves, and sesame oil until combined.
Pour 1 cup of the mixture into a bowl or shallow dish. Add the chicken into the mixture and marinate (refrigerated) overnight. Cover the rest of the mixture and refrigerate.
Drain the chicken and discard the mixture from the bowl.
Coat a 13x9-inch pan with cooking spray. Place the chicken in the pan and bake, uncovered, at 350°F for 30-40 minutes.
Meanwhile, combine the coleslaw, edamame, and chopped bell pepper in a large bowl.
Add peanut butter and salt to the remaining mixture and stir until combined. Pour over coleslaw mixture and toss well. Refrigerate until serving.
Serve the chicken with the salad. Top with green onions.
